title: What's new in income tax folios - Canada.ca
content:
## What's new in income tax folios
Income tax folios are a series of technical publications that reflect the Canada Revenue Agency's interpretation of federal income tax provisions as they relate to various topics. They were introduced in March 2013 and are gradually replacing income tax interpretation bulletins. To see the complete list of folios, visit the Income tax folio index page.

## Activity in the last twelve months
S3-F4-C1, General Discussion of Capital Cost Allowance
S5-F2-C1, Foreign Tax Credit
S4-F7-C1, Amalgamations of Canadian Corporations
S1-F1-C1, Medical Expense Tax Credit
S2-F2-C1, Employee Professional Membership and Other Dues
- Introducing income tax folios
- What the “Archived Content” notice means for interpretation bulletins
- Cancellation of income tax technical publications
